features.summary: Caterpillar reported strong Q2 2021 results with sales up 29% to $12.9 billion and adjusted EPS of $2.60 versus $1.27 prior year. Operating profit increased 128% to $1.8 billion with adjusted operating margin improving 480 basis points to 14.1%. Sales to users accelerated to 15% growth, driven by strong demand across all segments and regions. The company generated $1.7 billion in ME&T free cash flow and resumed share repurchases while increasing the dividend 8%. Supply chain challenges were modest but are expected to persist throughout the year.
features.management_outlook: Management expects continued strengthening in end-user demand with Q3 sales to users growth significantly higher than the 15% seen in Q2. They anticipate achieving Investor Day targets for adjusted operating profit margins and ME&T free cash flow of $4-8 billion for 2021. Construction demand should remain strong led by North America, while mining is expected to continue gradual improvement supported by commodity prices. The company doesn't expect significant dealer restocking benefits in 2021 and will continue prioritizing meeting end-user demand over inventory rebuilding.
features.risks: Supply chain disruptions are expected to remain challenging throughout 2021, though the company believes it can minimize production impacts. Material and freight cost inflation will pressure margins in the second half, particularly affecting Energy & Transportation segment which didn't implement price increases. Third quarter margins are expected to moderate sequentially due to higher manufacturing costs, reduced geographic mix benefits, and increased SG&A and R&D spending as business normalizes. China construction market faces tougher comparisons after strong 2020 performance.
features.key_drivers: Strong end-user demand recovery across all regions and segments is the primary growth driver, with construction benefiting from residential strength and improving non-residential activity. Mining demand is improving due to high commodity prices, increased utilization, and low parked truck levels. The company's autonomous mining solutions are deployed across 18 sites with strong customer interest. Energy transition trends are expected to drive long-term mining demand, while the company is investing in zero-emission and sustainable product development to meet customer environmental goals.
